description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a cream-colored fuselage and two sets of straight, parallel wings connected by struts and rigging wires. The aircraft features a single wooden propeller mounted at the nose, and an open cockpit situated just behind the upper wing. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer, both with rounded edges. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft, with visible rivets and a fabric-covered structure.
description: A cream-colored vintage biplane with dual wings, open cockpit, wooden propeller, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual wings, central propeller, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_1: Top oblique view highlighting the upper and lower wings, open cockpit, and tail structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the open cockpit.
description_view_3: Top oblique view from the opposite side, showing wing struts, cockpit, and tail details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by struts and rigging; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer, both with rounded edges.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the upper wing, likely single-seat with no canopy.
